title: PC Management Best Practices: A Study of the Total Cost of Ownership, Risk, Security, and Audit (IIA Research Foundation feature)
author: The IIA Research Foundation (RF Report, Issue No. 6)
abstract: The Institute of Internal Auditors Research Foundation biannual RF Report (February 2004, Issue No. 6) featuring the Foundation's new research report 'PC Management Best Practices: A Study of the Total Cost of Ownership, Risk, Security, and Audit' by Mark Salamasick and Charles Le Grand (2003). Peter Kastner (EVP and chief research officer, Aberdeen Group) is quoted from a technology forum: vulnerable machines may be called negligence; older PCs actually cost more to operate; and the optimal desktop lifecycle is three years, matching the duration of a standard warranty.
importance_rationale: Anchors Aberdeen/Kastner three-year PC lifecycle thesis in the Sarbanes-Oxley risk/audit context; IIA Research Foundation distribution reached audit and compliance practitioners.
relevance_rationale: TCO and desktop lifecycle arguments remain active in endpoint management; three-year refresh cycle remains widely referenced; SOX-IT framing is still foundational.
prescience_rationale: Three-year desktop lifecycle became the de facto corporate standard through the 2010s; PC lifecycle length is lengthening again due to cloud/SaaS shift, but three years remained dominant for ~15 years.
